2024-08-15 - 4 SaaS ideas to build in 20.title: 4 SaaS ideas to build in 2025 (and how to grow them)
2024-08-15 - 4 SaaS ideas to build in 20.executive_summary: Four SaaS ideas positioned for 2025 growth, each addressing markets that are either newly enabled by AI or experiencing structural shifts that create demand for new software. The episode covers not just the product ideas but comprehensive go-to-market strategies for each, including content-led growth, community-building, and partnership-driven distribution. Each idea is designed to be buildable by a solo founder or small team.
key_takeaways: SaaS ideas for 2025 should be AI-native: don't add AI to existing software categories, instead identify entirely new workflows that AI makes possible for the first time
Content-led growth is the most capital-efficient SaaS distribution strategy: create educational content about the problem your SaaS solves, build an audience, then launch the product
Partnership-driven distribution: integrate with complementary tools and platforms to access their existing user base rather than building your own from scratch
opportunities: AI-native SaaS tools for workflows that didn't exist before LLMs: automated research synthesis, intelligent document comparison, AI-powered competitive intelligence
SaaS products with built-in content distribution: the product itself generates content (reports, analyses, insights) that users share, creating organic growth loops
quotes: Don't add AI to old SaaS. Build new SaaS that only exists because of AI.

2024-08-23 - How I use Pinterest and Goo.episode_key: 2024-08-23 - How I use Pinterest and Google to find $1M+ startup ideas
2024-08-23 - How I use Pinterest and Goo.title: How I use Pinterest and Google to find $1M+ startup ideas
2024-08-23 - How I use Pinterest and Goo.executive_summary: Greg reveals his personal process for using Pinterest and Google as startup idea discovery tools, demonstrating how visual platforms and search data expose consumer demand that traditional market research misses. Pinterest's role as a visual search engine reveals what people aspire to buy and do, while Google's autocomplete and related searches expose the specific language and pain points of real customers. The methodology is designed to find million-dollar opportunities in consumer and prosumer markets.
key_takeaways: Pinterest is a leading indicator of consumer demand: what people pin today is what they'll buy in 6-12 months -- use it as a trend forecasting tool
Google autocomplete reveals exact customer language: build your marketing copy, landing pages, and product names using the words your customers actually search for
Combine visual demand signals (Pinterest) with search intent data (Google) to validate ideas with both aspiration and urgency
opportunities: Pinterest trend analysis tools: build products or content targeting the top rising Pinterest categories before they peak in mainstream awareness
SEO-optimized niche businesses: use Google's autocomplete data to identify underserved long-tail search queries and build content or products targeting them
quotes: Pinterest tells you what people dream about. Google tells you what they're desperate for. The overlap is where the money is.

2024-08-27 - 5 validated startup ideas t.episode_key: 2024-08-27 - 5 validated startup ideas that will make you money
2024-08-27 - 5 validated startup ideas t.title: 5 validated startup ideas that will make you money
2024-08-27 - 5 validated startup ideas t.executive_summary: Five pre-validated startup ideas presented with existing proof of demand, revenue potential, and competitive gaps. Each idea has been validated through customer interviews, market data analysis, or observation of adjacent successful businesses. The episode focuses on reducing startup risk by choosing ideas where demand is already proven and the challenge is purely execution.
key_takeaways: Validated ideas have three forms of proof: existing businesses doing it poorly (competitor validation), customers actively searching for solutions (demand validation), or adjacent markets where the model already works (model validation)
Execution risk is more manageable than market risk: choose validated markets where the question is 'can you build it better?' rather than 'does anyone want this?'
Speed to market with validated ideas: since the market is proven, the competitive advantage goes to whoever executes fastest with the best experience
opportunities: Better-executed versions of existing products in validated markets: identify products with high usage but low satisfaction and build superior alternatives
Market-validated service businesses that can be launched in days rather than months
quotes: The safest startup is one where customers are already paying someone else for a worse version of what you're building.

2024-08-29 - The step by step guide to s.episode_key: 2024-08-29 - The step by step guide to starting a business from $0
2024-08-29 - The step by step guide to s.title: The step by step guide to starting a business from $0
2024-08-29 - The step by step guide to s.executive_summary: A comprehensive guide for starting a business with zero capital, covering every step from idea selection to first revenue. The episode challenges the myth that you need money to make money, demonstrating how skills, hustle, and strategic use of free tools can replace capital in the early stages. The guide covers idea validation, MVP creation using no-code tools, customer acquisition through organic channels, and the transition from side project to full-time business.
key_takeaways: Starting from $0 is about leveraging time and skills instead of capital: every business function (website, marketing, sales) has free or near-free tools available
The $0 to first $1K is the hardest part: focus exclusively on getting your first paying customer before anything else -- no logo, no website, no business cards needed
Free tools stack: Carrd for landing pages, Stripe for payments, Twitter/LinkedIn for distribution, Notion for operations, and ChatGPT for content creation
opportunities: Service-first businesses that require zero capital: consulting, freelancing, or productized services where your expertise is the product
No-code SaaS products built with tools like Bubble, Softr, or Airtable that can generate revenue before any custom development
quotes: You don't need money to start a business. You need one customer who trusts you enough to pay you.

2024-09-04 - 4 profitable startups from.episode_key: 2024-09-04 - 4 profitable startups from the founder of exploding topics
2024-09-04 - 4 profitable startups from.title: 4 profitable startups from the founder of exploding topics
2024-09-04 - 4 profitable startups from.executive_summary: The founder of Exploding Topics shares four profitable startup ideas informed by trend data analysis. Each idea is backed by specific data showing rapid growth in search volume, social mention frequency, or market spending. The episode demonstrates how systematic trend monitoring can identify business opportunities months or years before they become mainstream, providing a structural advantage over founders who rely on intuition alone.
key_takeaways: Trend data provides a structural advantage: founders who systematically monitor emerging trends identify opportunities 6-18 months before competitors who rely on intuition
The best trend-based businesses are 'trend-adjacent': don't build the trending thing itself, build the tools and services that serve people who are riding the trend
Profitable startups emerge at the intersection of rising trends and underserved markets: when demand is growing faster than supply, pricing power is natural
opportunities: Trend-adjacent infrastructure businesses: build tools, services, or content that serve industries experiencing rapid growth
Data-as-a-service for specific verticals: package trend monitoring and market intelligence for industry verticals where professionals pay premium prices for information advantage
quotes: The best time to start a trend-based business is when the trend line is going up but before your mom has heard of it.

2024-10-07 - How Creators Can Thrive in.title: How Creators Can Thrive in the AI + AR Era
2024-10-07 - How Creators Can Thrive in.executive_summary: An exploration of how creators can adapt and thrive as AI and augmented reality reshape the content landscape. The episode covers strategies for creators to use AI as a productivity multiplier rather than viewing it as a threat, opportunities in AR content creation, and how to build creator businesses that are AI-resistant. Key theme: creators who combine uniquely human qualities (perspective, taste, relationships) with AI capabilities will dramatically outperform those who rely on either alone.
key_takeaways: AI-resistant creator businesses combine three elements: unique perspective (can't be replicated by AI), community relationships (built on trust over time), and multi-format presence (diversified across platforms)
AR creates entirely new content categories: spatial content, interactive experiences, and location-based media are greenfield opportunities for early-adopting creators
Use AI to 10x your output while keeping your voice: AI handles production tasks (editing, formatting, distributing) while you focus on the ideas and relationships that make you unique
opportunities: AR content creation tools and templates for creators who want to experiment with spatial computing without deep technical expertise
AI-powered creator studios: end-to-end production platforms that help creators produce 10x more content across all formats from a single recording session
quotes: AI won't replace creators. But creators who use AI will replace creators who don't.

2024-10-18 - Do this to build a $100k/mo.executive_summary: A tactical guide to building a $100K/month Chrome extension using AI coding tools like Cursor AI and Claude. The key insight: AI coding assistants have reduced Chrome extension development time from months to days, making it possible for non-technical founders to build profitable extensions.
key_takeaways: Chrome extensions are an underrated business model: high distribution potential through the Web Store, sticky usage patterns, and freemium monetization works exceptionally well
AI coding tools (Cursor AI, Claude) enable non-technical founders to build functional Chrome extensions in days: the bottleneck has shifted from coding ability to idea quality
Study the Chrome Web Store for opportunities: look for extensions with high install counts but poor reviews, then build better versions with modern UX and AI capabilities

2024-10-21 - Do this to build a $200,000.title: Do this to build a $200,000 side hustle (The Online Directory Blueprint)
2024-10-21 - Do this to build a $200,000.executive_summary: The complete blueprint for building an online directory that generates $200K+ as a side hustle. The episode covers niche selection (look for industries with fragmented local businesses and no dominant directory), SEO strategies for ranking directory pages, and monetization through premium listings, lead generation fees, and sponsored placements. Key insight: directories are boring but incredibly profitable because they compound in value as content and backlinks accumulate over time.
key_takeaways: Online directories are SEO powerhouses: each listing creates a unique page that can rank for long-tail keywords, compounding organic traffic over time
Monetize directories through three channels: premium listings ($50-500/month), lead generation fees ($5-50 per qualified lead), and sponsored placements for key categories
Pick niches with fragmented local businesses (contractors, clinics, studios) that don't have a dominant Yelp or Google Maps presence in their specific category
opportunities: Niche industry directories for underserved verticals: AI consultants, home service specialists, wellness practitioners, or specialty retailers
AI-enhanced directory platforms: use AI to automatically verify listings, generate descriptions, and match users with the right providers
quotes: Directories are the most boring business on the internet. That's exactly why they're so profitable.

2024-10-23 - 2 $1M+ EdTech Startups you.episode_key: 2024-10-23 - 2 $1M+ EdTech Startups you can launch tomorrow
2024-10-23 - 2 $1M+ EdTech Startups you.title: 2 $1M+ EdTech Startups you can launch tomorrow
2024-10-23 - 2 $1M+ EdTech Startups you.executive_summary: Two million-dollar EdTech startup ideas that can be launched immediately, targeting the growing gap between traditional education and the skills demanded by the modern economy. The episode covers how AI is creating new categories of educational products that didn't exist before: personalized learning paths, skill-based micro-credentials, and AI tutoring systems. Each idea is positioned at the intersection of rising demand for continuous learning and the decreasing cost of delivering personalized education at scale.
key_takeaways: EdTech's biggest opportunity is in adult professional development, not K-12: professionals are willing to pay premium prices for skills that directly increase their earning potential
AI tutoring enables personalized learning at scale for the first time: one-on-one instruction quality at one-to-many economics
Micro-credentials and skill badges are replacing traditional certifications: build platforms that validate specific skills through practical assessments rather than seat time

2024-10-28 - How to make $20,000,000 wit.episode_key: 2024-10-28 - How to make $20,000,000 with a landing page
2024-10-28 - How to make $20,000,000 wit.title: How to make $20,000,000 with a landing page
2024-10-28 - How to make $20,000,000 wit.executive_summary: Jonathan Courtney, CEO of AJ&Smart, reveals how a single ugly funnel generated over $20M in revenue across 3.5 years, demonstrating that conversion-optimized funnels dramatically outperform polished websites. The episode contrasts websites (1-3% conversion) with funnels (10-25% conversion) and introduces the MIFGE (Most Incredible Free Gift Ever) strategy used by brands like AG1 and Eight Sleep. Key insight: funnels and websites should be completely separate properties, with funnels focused exclusively on one CTA.
key_takeaways: Build dedicated funnels separate from your website - ugly, copy-heavy funnels consistently outperform beautiful branded pages for revenue generation
Use the MIFGE (Most Incredible Free Gift Ever) strategy to stack one-time bonuses onto offers, dramatically increasing conversion rates even for high-ticket products
Focus your entire content team on driving traffic to one funnel with one goal (e.g., call bookings), rather than spreading across multiple destinations
opportunities: VSL (Video Sales Letter) funnel agency for bootstrapped businesses selling high-ticket services ($7K-$14K range)
Book funnel as lead generation - physical books drive disproportionate engagement and user-generated marketing content
quotes: If you aren't disgusted with your funnel, you've launched too late

2024-10-30 - How to steal funnels that c.episode_key: 2024-10-30 - How to steal funnels that crush
2024-10-30 - How to steal funnels that c.title: How to steal funnels that crush
2024-10-30 - How to steal funnels that c.executive_summary: Jordan, operating partner at Late Checkout, teaches how to build a 'swipe file' - a curated vault of proven marketing winners that transforms passive content consumption into future revenue. The episode walks through real examples of scroll-stopping creative (fried chicken ice cream, Colin & Samir thumbnails) and IRL-to-URL lead magnets, demonstrating how the best marketers systematically study how they get sold. A concrete micro-SaaS idea emerges: an AI agent that scrapes and catalogs entire marketing funnels automatically.
key_takeaways: Build a systematic swipe file using tools like MyMind - tag everything that 'got you' (made you click, subscribe, or buy) to turn consumption into curation
Study how you personally get sold to understand conversion psychology - the best way to learn selling is analyzing what worked on you
In your funnel, top-of-funnel content should be about THEM, nurture adds YOU into their lives, and conversion is where you earn the right to talk about yourself
opportunities: AI agent funnel scraper - a tool that automatically maps email sequences, landing pages, and conversion flows from competitors into a searchable database
Pay-what-you-want pricing model for SaaS products, following Sublime app's innovative approach
quotes: The best way to learn how to sell is studying how you got sold

2024-11-01 - Fire your team and hire AI.episode_key: 2024-11-01 - Fire your team and hire AI employees?
2024-11-01 - Fire your team and hire AI.title: Fire your team and hire AI employees?
2024-11-01 - Fire your team and hire AI.executive_summary: Flo, CEO of Lindy.ai, demonstrates live how to build AI employees that handle meeting recording, email triage, scheduling, proposal writing, and design recruiting - all through natural language prompts. The key differentiator from Zapier is intelligence: Lindy agents maintain context across long conversations, understand nuance, and can delegate tasks to other agents. The episode showcases multi-agent orchestration where one Lindy delegates proposal writing to another, previewing the future of autonomous AI workforces.
key_takeaways: Start by mapping your 'jobs to be done' - identify every repetitive task in your business, then create individual AI agents (Lindies) for each before connecting them together
AI agents differ from automations (Zapier) in two critical ways: AI prompts can be used at any step, and agents manage long-term tasks with full conversational context
The business of the future is one person with an army of 2,000+ AI agents that scale elastically with demand - paying nothing when idle, scaling instantly during peaks
opportunities: Build a recruiting company powered entirely by AI agents - one person managing an army of AI recruiters that find candidates, verify emails, and send personalized outreach
AI-powered customer support that scales elastically for seasonal businesses (e.g., Black Friday spikes) without hiring temporary staff
quotes: The business of the future is basically you can set up a team of 5,000 AI employees in a weekend

2024-11-04 - 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you.episode_key: 2024-11-04 - 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you can launch tomorrow with $0
2024-11-04 - 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you.title: 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you can launch tomorrow with $0
2024-11-04 - 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you.executive_summary: Cody Schneider presents seven validated AI startup ideas with distribution strategies, emphasizing that AI enables 10x ad creative volume at 1/100th the cost. Key ideas include AI UGC ad generation (replacing $500/ad with $0.99 AI avatars), AI infographic generators with exact-match domains, AI journaling apps with high retention, and AI receptionists for vertical markets like dentists. The throughline: don't invent new categories - take existing behaviors people already pay for and make them 10x faster/cheaper with AI.
key_takeaways: Run A-to-Z testing instead of A/B testing - create 100+ ad variations simultaneously using AI-generated UGC to identify winning creatives at 1/100th traditional cost

Prioritize startup ideas by usage frequency - daily/weekly cadence apps (journaling, infographics) dramatically outperform one-time-use tools (logo generators, business plan writers) in retention and revenue
AI receptionists for vertical markets (dentists, barbershops) represent a massive opportunity - labor shortages make businesses receptive, and APIs like Vapi make building them accessible
opportunities: AI UGC ad agency - produce unlimited AI-avatar content variations for e-commerce brands at a fraction of traditional UGC creator costs
AI receptionist SaaS for specific verticals (medical, dental, restaurants) - build a wrapper around voice APIs and sell directly to local businesses
quotes: Don't be creative - find a category that has existed forever and will exist forever, then remix it with new technology

2024-11-06 - 24 year old making $1m/mont.episode_key: 2024-11-06 - 24 year old making $1m/month from AI shares 3 startup ideas
2024-11-06 - 24 year old making $1m/mont.title: 24 year old making $1m/month from AI shares 3 startup ideas
2024-11-06 - 24 year old making $1m/mont.executive_summary: Blake Anderson (23), founder of Cal AI ($1M+/month in revenue) and Umax (10M+ downloads), shares three AI app ideas: Career AI (all-in-one job advancement platform), Journal AI (emotional support journaling with memory), and a Duolingo competitor using real-time AI tutoring. Develop personal relationships with influencers (DM every platform, message their family if needed) rather than relying on marketplace databases - relationship-driven outreach dramatically outperforms cold approaches
Optimize for users^N (N>1) x profit-per-user rather than users x profit - accepting lower LTV per download in exchange for faster market dominance and unmeasurable word-of-mouth growth
opportunities: Career AI - an all-in-one platform combining resume analysis, career quizzes, and job application tools with gamified scoring (like Umax's 6-factor rating) for viral TikTok distribution
Language learning AI app - capture even 1% of Duolingo's market by iterating on AI features faster than incumbents whose engineers lack aligned incentives

Target high-LTV demographics often overlooked on TikTok (golfers, poker players) where there's less creator competition but high willingness to pay for apps
Build apps that serve as utilities within existing TikTok Shop creator content - e.g., a skincare analysis app that helps beauty creators demonstrate product results, giving you free distribution
opportunities: Golf swing analysis app using GPT Vision - $70K/month revenue potential with 10K downloads targeting a demographic willing to pay $125 for in-person alternatives
Capitalize on cult movements (looksmaxing, wealth-building) by creating the definitive app for that community with built-in viral shareability

Silent code refactoring as a service would continuously scan, optimize, and test code improvements in sandboxed environments - start at $50/month for linting/error tracking and scale to enterprise
Build niche data aggregation businesses at the intersection of your existing expertise - a horse person building horse industry intelligence, for example, can speak the language and build trust

Niche industry intelligence newsletters with per-subscriber personalization - deliver the same source material as bullet points, long-form email, AI podcast, or video depending on each reader's preference

2024-11-25 - 3 startup ideas from the co.episode_key: 2024-11-25 - 3 startup ideas from the co-founder of Loom (make $120k/mo)
2024-11-25 - 3 startup ideas from the co.title: 3 startup ideas from the co-founder of Loom (make $120k/mo)
2024-11-25 - 3 startup ideas from the co.executive_summary: Shahid, co-founder of Loom (sold for ~$1B), shares three ideas born from personal frustration: an anti-TripAdvisor travel guide app curating hidden gems from TikTok micro-influencers; a 'Path for 2025' micro social network limited to 150 close friends showing real-time locations (Dunbar's number); and ephemeral time capsule gifts that package shared photo memories for physical delivery. The unifying thesis: millennials crave curation, intimacy, and nostalgia - all underserved by current platforms.

Clone the best elements of Path (2013 social network, sold for ~$100M) for today's market - limit to 150 users, charge $20-30/year, and leverage PR around anti-social-media positioning
Nostalgia is a monetizable emotion - shared albums are where memories go to die, creating an opportunity for curated physical time capsule gifts paired with ephemeral digital experiences

Dunbar social network - a private, location-sharing micro social network capped at 150 friends, monetized through subscriptions and anonymized data partnerships

2024-11-27 - How This Guy Makes His Enti.episode_key: 2024-11-27 - How This Guy Makes His Entire Year's Revenue in Black Friday WEEK
2024-11-27 - How This Guy Makes His Enti.title: How This Guy Makes His Entire Year's Revenue in Black Friday WEEK
2024-11-27 - How This Guy Makes His Enti.executive_summary: Jonathan Courtney dissects elite Black Friday strategies being used by brands like Apple, AG1, Magic Mind, and Four Sigmatic, revealing that a well-executed campaign week can generate an entire year's revenue. Key tactics include: Apple's $200 gift voucher approach (discount without cheapening the brand), escalating MIFGE gifts throughout the week, store credit framing ($21.63 credit vs. 20% off), and Four Sigmatic's gamified build-your-own-bundle experience. The biggest missed opportunity: SaaS companies ignoring seasonal campaigns entirely.
key_takeaways: Frame discounts as store credit with specific odd amounts ($21.63 instead of 20% off) to create urgency and perceived ownership - this dramatically outperforms standard percentage discounts
Start planning Black Friday campaigns in June, not November - escalate free gifts throughout the week, adding new bonuses for late buyers while honoring early purchasers
A dedicated Black Friday campaign agency is a viable business - reach out to e-commerce companies in August, take a revenue cut instead of a flat fee, and proactively build their entire campaign
opportunities: Black Friday campaign agency - proactively reach out to e-commerce brands offering to run their entire holiday campaign for a revenue share, no flat fees
Shopify app for gamified build-your-own-bundle experiences with progressive discount checkpoints (like Four Sigmatic's model)
quotes: If you can do a great Black Friday sale, you can literally mess up everything for the rest of the year and still have a great year

2024-12-02 - Build a high traffic startu.title: Build a high traffic startup using AI, Webflow and Zapier
2024-12-02 - Build a high traffic startu.executive_summary: Omar demonstrates how he built two AI-powered directory sites - Guilty Chef (recipes) and Best Dubai (travel) - that compete with TripAdvisor and rank #1 on Google, all as side projects using Webflow, Zapier, Apify, and Claude. AI-powered launch automation tool that orchestrates email sequences, ad rotations, and landing page variants based on proven launch frameworks

2025-08-30 - Using Google Ads, Heygen AI.title: Using Google Ads, Heygen AI, Perplexity to make you $$$
2025-08-30 - Using Google Ads, Heygen AI.executive_summary: Cody Schneider delivers a hands-on masterclass for bootstrapped founders on customer acquisition through Google Ads, Facebook Ads, and AI-generated video content. The episode walks through keyword research with Keywords Everywhere, setting up click-to-conversion campaigns on Google, and creating UGC-style Facebook ads using HeyGen avatars and ElevenLabs voices. Key insight: most indie hackers irrationally avoid paid ads despite favorable unit economics, and low search volume niches (100 searches/month) can sustain profitable micro-SaaS businesses.
key_takeaways: Validate demand before building: use keyword research and Reddit threads to confirm people are searching for your solution before writing a single line of code
Start Google Ads with click campaigns first, then graduate to signup conversions, then payment conversions as data accumulates - you need volume to train the algorithm
AI avatar ads (HeyGen + ElevenLabs) combined with Instagram Trial Reels provide a near-zero-cost testing ground for ad creative before committing budget
opportunities: AI avatar ad creation agency serving vibe-coded SaaS products that need customer acquisition
Data extraction micro-SaaS tools targeting niche keyword families with 100-500 monthly searches
quotes: Apps that have a 100 search volume a month, I've paid my rent for the last 10 years. Like I will never talk about these in public.

2025-09-03 - I quit my job to make $6M/y.title: I quit my job to make $6M/year with AI apps
2025-09-03 - I quit my job to make $6M/y.executive_summary: Ben, the creator of Wombo (100M+ downloads), shares his playbook for building viral AI-powered mobile apps: identify open-source AI models gaining traction in technical circles, wrap them in dead-simple UIs, and engineer the output for shareability. The conversation covers his journey from zero to 250M installs, surviving a near-fatal funding collapse, and pivoting toward decentralized compute with W.ai. His core thesis: copy what works, put your own spin on it, and optimize for the content creation loop.
key_takeaways: When an AI model goes viral in technical communities but is hard for laypeople to use, wrapping it in a simple mobile app is a proven recipe for massive adoption
Design apps for a viral content creation loop: user creates content, shares it on social platforms, which drives new users back to the app organically
Monetize with weekly subscriptions ($3-$20/week), knowing that 98% of users will be free and drive virality while 2% generate all revenue
opportunities: AI mobile apps that productize trending content formats (e.g., turning celebrities into animated characters, baby-fying photos)
Decentralized compute networks that let consumer devices contribute idle GPU/CPU cycles for AI inference
quotes: If you make something truly great and truly unique and kind of truly captures the current moment, then I think you can get hundreds of millions of downloads and make at least tens of millions of dollars just with popcorn mobile apps.

2025-09-08 - Screensharing How This Guy.episode_key: 2025-09-08 - Screensharing How This Guy Makes Millions with AI Agents (Rowan Cheung)
2025-09-08 - Screensharing How This Guy.title: Screensharing How This Guy Makes Millions with AI Agents (Rowan Cheung)
2025-09-08 - Screensharing How This Guy.executive_summary: Rowan Cheung, founder of The Rundown AI newsletter, reveals seven AI workflows that allow his 15-person team to operate like a 50-person organization. Demonstrations include AI avatar content creation (HeyGen + ElevenLabs), voice-dictated tweet writing with Claude, a Lindy-powered scheduling agent that responds to leads in under 60 seconds, a custom GPT for employee onboarding, and a Zapier agent that filters support emails and routes them to the right team via Slack. The episode provides copy-paste templates for each workflow.
key_takeaways: Train Claude on your top-performing content to create a voice-matched writing assistant - use it as an editor-in-chief rather than a first-draft generator for highest quality output
Deploy an AI scheduling agent (Lindy) that responds to inbound leads within 60 seconds, 24/7 - speed-to-response directly correlates with conversion rates in services businesses
Build a custom GPT loaded with SOPs, HR policies, and team directories to onboard new employees without burdening managers or creating information bottlenecks
opportunities: AI-powered executive assistant services for founders who need 24/7 scheduling, email triage, and lead qualification without hiring human EAs
Template marketplace for Claude/GPT writing styles trained on high-performing content in specific niches
quotes: Sometimes it would be faster if I just came in and quickly narrated it, but I don't even have that 10 minutes of time and that break in the workflow every single day takes a lot of mental capacity.

2025-09-10 - This Solopreneur Called Me.episode_key: 2025-09-10 - This Solopreneur Called Me to Fix His $20k/mo SaaS
2025-09-10 - This Solopreneur Called Me.title: This Solopreneur Called Me to Fix His $20k/mo SaaS
2025-09-10 - This Solopreneur Called Me.executive_summary: A live consulting session where Greg Isenberg and marketer Gabe diagnose the retention problems of PostBridge, a $20K MRR social media scheduling SaaS run by solopreneur Jack Ficks. The analysis reveals five strategic gaps: no community, weak brand positioning, absence from YouTube, no proprietary analytics metric, and limited API/MCP integrations. The episode provides a universally applicable framework for reducing churn through world-building, contextual onboarding, gamification, and converting monthly subscribers to annual plans via email marketing.
key_takeaways: Build a community around your product and include it as a subscription perk - users who connect with other users have dramatically lower churn rates
Create a proprietary metric unique to your product (like VidIQ's 'outlier score') to differentiate in a crowded market and lock users into your ecosystem
Use customer call transcripts fed into ChatGPT to discover messaging patterns and rewrite your positioning using customers' own language
opportunities: Gamification-as-a-service agency that helps SaaS products add streaks, leaderboards, and time-saved metrics to reduce churn
MCP integration layer for scheduling tools enabling agentic posting from AI workflows
quotes: I always look at everything as like what's the MVP to this? Like maybe it's not a new video every week, but maybe it's like once a month or once every two months even, like just something.

2025-09-12 - AI Search is PRINTING (Chat.episode_key: 2025-09-12 - AI Search is PRINTING (ChatGPT, GEO & Perplexity)
2025-09-12 - AI Search is PRINTING (Chat.title: AI Search is PRINTING (ChatGPT, GEO & Perplexity)
2025-09-12 - AI Search is PRINTING (Chat.executive_summary: Cody Schneider breaks down Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) - the practice of getting your brand cited by AI chatbots like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini. He explains the mechanics: AI models fan out user queries into 100+ derivative searches, scrape the top-ranking Google pages, and synthesize answers. Brands that appear on more of these source URLs get cited more often. While conversion rates from AI search traffic are 10-40% (vs. 1-2% from traditional search), total volume remains small, making this a supplement rather than replacement for SEO.
key_takeaways: AI search works by scraping Google pages 1-3 for source material - your job is to get your brand mentioned on as many of those ranking URLs as possible through outreach and link placement
Use AI SEO tracker tools to find which URLs are being referenced for queries related to your product, then systematically contact those site owners to get included
GEO delivers overnight results unlike traditional SEO, but is vulnerable to model updates - treat it as one channel in your stack, not your entire strategy
opportunities: AI search optimization agency that identifies source URLs, manages outreach campaigns, and tracks brand mention percentages across LLMs
Listicle placement marketplace connecting brands seeking AI search visibility with publishers ranking on relevant queries
quotes: If organic search is working for you, paid search will work for you. I guarantee it. 99 out of 100 times it will work. It's a skill issue.

2025-09-17 - Apps make $50K/mo like THIS.episode_key: 2025-09-17 - Apps make $50K/mo like THIS (I shouldn't share this)
2025-09-17 - Apps make $50K/mo like THIS.title: Apps make $50K/mo like THIS (I shouldn't share this)
2025-09-17 - Apps make $50K/mo like THIS.executive_summary: Cody Schneider reveals the creator marketing playbook that consumer apps use to achieve cheaper-than-paid-ads CPMs on TikTok and Instagram. The key distinction: creators (zero followers, paid $900/month for daily posts) outperform influencers ($2K for one post) because for-you-page algorithms reward content quality over follower count. The strategy involves hiring 5-10 creators, testing 30+ videos monthly, sharing winning formats across the team, and converting top organic performers into paid ads.
key_takeaways: Hire creators at $900/month for a post per day across all short-form platforms - at 500K aggregate impressions, you achieve a $2 CPM vs. $6-8 on paid channels
Work with 5-10 creators simultaneously to create a compound learning flywheel: share winning formats across the team and double down on what works
Promote your best-performing creator to team manager to handle logistics, quality control, and format distribution while continuing their own posting
opportunities: Creator management platform specifically for brand-creator relationships (scheduling, format sharing, performance tracking, payment)
Creator-as-a-service offering for vibe-coded consumer apps that need short-form distribution but lack marketing expertise
quotes: If I can pay a dollar and then that turns into $5 in customer lifetime value - that is all ads are when you set it up. It's like that is literally the game here.

2025-09-19 - My AI agents scrape Google.episode_key: 2025-09-19 - My AI agents scrape Google Maps to make $$ in BORING businesses
2025-09-19 - My AI agents scrape Google.title: My AI agents scrape Google Maps to make $$ in BORING businesses
2025-09-19 - My AI agents scrape Google.executive_summary: James, aka The Boring Marketer, demonstrates an n8n workflow that scrapes Google Maps to validate boring local business opportunities. The workflow collects review volume, review velocity, sentiment analysis, competitor density, and social presence for any niche in any city. His thesis: target underserved micro-niches (hardscaping, closet redesign, garage renovation) in tier-2 cities where demand outstrips supply. Rather than starting the service business directly, he advocates building local media assets (newsletters, directories) to generate and sell leads.
key_takeaways: Use Google Maps review data as a market intelligence source: high review velocity plus low average ratings signals strong demand with poor supply - a clear arbitrage opportunity
Target tier-2 cities (Nashville, Charlotte, Denver) where population growth creates service demand that existing providers cannot meet
Build the media company first (local newsletter, directory) to own the customer relationship, then monetize via lead generation at $100-200 per qualified lead
opportunities: AI-powered local niche research tool that automates Google Maps scraping, sentiment analysis, and opportunity scoring for boring business ideas
Lead generation businesses in underserved local service niches (mobile diesel mechanics, smart home installation, exotic car detailing)
quotes: Nobody's really talking about it, which blows my mind. On my timeline, it's literally all the same things - HVAC, plumbing. And what's going to happen is you're going to have a bunch of people who have no business building in that space go into an extremely competitive space.

2025-09-22 - Making $100K/month with iOS.title: Making $100K/month with iOS apps (BREAKDOWN)
2025-09-22 - Making $100K/month with iOS.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg presents a comprehensive framework for building consumer mobile apps that reach $100K MRR. The playbook covers: selecting a daily habit to serve, validating demand on Reddit/TikTok, prototyping with Lovable/Bolt, distribution testing on one channel with three formats, pricing architecture (7-day free trial, $7-40/month), and scaling via ASO, affiliates, and paid ads. Four specific app ideas are pitched: dog allergy scanner, migraine weather guard, silent study timer, and plant watering AI coach.
key_takeaways: Target daily habits (not weekly/monthly use cases) and ensure there is evidence of paid demand - frequency drives retention and willingness to pay
Achieve the user's first win within the first hour of onboarding; every hour of delay represents an order of magnitude worse retention
Track weekly active paying users (WAPU) as your North Star metric, with LTV/CAC above 3x as the threshold for scaling paid acquisition
opportunities: Niche AI mobile apps that combine photo-scan functionality with personalized tracking (pet allergies, plant care, food safety)
Study productivity apps with gamified streaks, shared virtual rooms, and lo-fi music integration targeting exam prep communities
quotes: People think it's getting saturated with consumer mobile apps, but I don't think that's the case. There's going to be like a hundred mini versions of CalAI or more that are successful.

2025-10-04 - How I'd use Sora 2 + Claude.episode_key: 2025-10-04 - How I'd use Sora 2 + Claude to hit 1M+ views on AI videos
2025-10-04 - How I'd use Sora 2 + Claude.title: How I'd use Sora 2 + Claude to hit 1M+ views on AI videos
2025-10-04 - How I'd use Sora 2 + Claude.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg walks through a three-tool workflow for creating viral short-form video ads: Perplexity Deep Research for audience psychology analysis, Claude for generating and scoring 10 video concepts against engagement criteria, and Sora 2 for producing the final content. The key innovation is using Claude as both a concept generator and evaluation agent, rating each idea on hook strength, pattern interrupt, emotional triggers, and algorithm fit before generating optimized Sora prompts.
key_takeaways: Use Perplexity Deep Research to understand the psychological triggers of your target audience before generating any creative concepts
Have Claude evaluate and score each video concept (1-10) on hook strength, emotional trigger, and algorithm fit before investing in production
Prompt Claude to write Sora-optimized prompts rather than writing them yourself - this leverages Claude's understanding of Sora 2's capabilities for better output
opportunities: AI-powered creative brief generator that combines audience research, concept scoring, and platform-optimized prompt generation into a single workflow
Sora 2 ad creative agency specializing in scroll-stopping content for bootstrapped SaaS and consumer app founders
quotes: The people who are going to write incredible Sora prompts will have the power of Hollywood in their hands.

2025-10-08 - OpenAI's NEW AI Agent Build.title: OpenAI's NEW AI Agent Builder Replaces n8n & Zapier
2025-10-08 - OpenAI's NEW AI Agent Build.executive_summary: Amir demonstrates OpenAI's new Agent Builder, ChatKit SDK, and Widgets - three tools that enable non-technical users to build multi-agent workflows visually and deploy them as website chatbots. The live demo builds a customer classifier that routes inbound inquiries to either a support agent (trained on a vector store) or a sales lead capture agent, with configurable reasoning levels, guard rails for hallucination prevention, and MCP integrations for CRM updates. The episode positions this as a significant democratization of agentic AI.
key_takeaways: OpenAI's Agent Builder lets you visually create multi-agent workflows with parallel/sequential steps, tool calling, guard rails, and MCP integrations - no code required
Use minimal reasoning for simple data collection tasks (sales qualification) and high reasoning for complex problem-solving (support troubleshooting) to optimize speed and cost
ChatKit enables non-engineering teams to deploy and update chatbots on websites without developer dependency - changes publish directly from the Agent Builder
opportunities: Pre-built agent workflow templates for specific verticals (e-commerce support, SaaS onboarding, real estate lead qualification)
Agent Builder consulting services helping SMBs replace expensive chatbot subscriptions with custom OpenAI-powered solutions
quotes: What OpenAI has done with their new update is they've created a visual interface for you to actually build workflows using agents - it's essentially reducing the barrier for non-technical people to get started with building multi-agent workflows.

2025-10-15 - Genspark's Super AI Agent i.episode_key: 2025-10-15 - Genspark's Super AI Agent is INSANE
2025-10-15 - Genspark's Super AI Agent i.title: Genspark's Super AI Agent is INSANE
2025-10-15 - Genspark's Super AI Agent i.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg reviews GenSpark AI, the most-requested hidden gem AI app from his 500K+ X followers. The platform's killer feature is multi-agent mode: one prompt simultaneously queries GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4, and Gemini 2.5, then a reflection agent synthesizes the best response. The review covers image generation across multiple models, video creation with auto-prompting, AI slides with fact-checking, and AI sheets for lead research. At ~$20/month, GenSpark positions as a cheaper alternative to running multiple AI subscriptions.
key_takeaways: Multi-agent AI tools that query multiple LLMs simultaneously and reflect on outputs save significant time and token costs compared to manual LLM ping-pong
Auto-prompting features that optimize your inputs for each specific model dramatically improve output quality, especially for image and video generation
Use AI slide tools with fact-checking to create fundraising decks and lead magnets, then export to Google Slides for final polish
opportunities: Niche multi-agent AI platforms specialized for specific workflows (e.g., content creation, market research, investor decks)
Lead magnet automation pipeline: AI generates content, creates slides, exports to landing pages, collects emails
quotes: Instead of me having to go individually to those products, I have it right there. And the other thing that it should be doing is reflecting on what is the best output.

2025-10-20 - Make $1M+ in 2026 with this.episode_key: 2025-10-20 - Make $1M+ in 2026 with this Trend (IRL is back)
2025-10-20 - Make $1M+ in 2026 with this.title: Make $1M+ in 2026 with this Trend (IRL is back)
2025-10-20 - Make $1M+ in 2026 with this.executive_summary: Jonathan Courtney of AJ&Smart argues that the biggest business opportunity in 2026 is the anti-AI trend: in-person experiences. Social media usage peaked in 2022 among young people, creating massive demand for IRL events that feel like an escape from digital overload. Jonathan shares how he sold 30 tickets at $6,800 each for a 'Summer Camp' mastermind and $14,300 certifications that outsell their $6,000 online equivalents. The framework: take popular digital-only products, create themed in-person versions, leverage natural scarcity, and use the community to fund software products.
key_takeaways: In-person events have inherent scarcity that makes marketing dramatically easier than digital products - limited tickets create genuine urgency without artificial FOMO tactics
Price in-person experiences 2-3x higher than digital equivalents and lead with the human connection value proposition - people are craving real-world interaction
Use IRL events and communities as the top of funnel to build relationships, then vibe code SaaS products and sell them to the community you've already built
opportunities: Premium in-person mastermind events in any niche where digital-only communities currently dominate (marketing, AI, investing, health)
IRL community-funded SaaS model: build the community first through events, validate product ideas in person, then sell software to members
quotes: Scarcity is really hard to do with a digital product. In-person events actually have a limited amount of tickets. So it's way easier to create a true sense of urgency and scarcity.

2025-10-27 - Claude Skills Built Me an A.episode_key: 2025-10-27 - Claude Skills Built Me an AI Agent Army (They Run Everything Now)
2025-10-27 - Claude Skills Built Me an A.title: Claude Skills Built Me an AI Agent Army (They Run Everything Now)
2025-10-27 - Claude Skills Built Me an A.executive_summary: Amir explains Claude Skills - a feature that creates reusable, laser-focused AI workflows with custom instructions, reference files, and executable scripts. Unlike projects (broad workspaces) or sub-agents (conversation-scoped), skills load context only when relevant, reducing hallucination from context overload. Live demos include building a UTM link generator artifact, an A/B testing analyzer, and a marketing insights agent. The episode argues that skills solve the #1 complaint about enterprise AI adoption: inconsistent and incorrect output.
key_takeaways: Claude Skills solve context rot by loading only task-relevant context rather than overwhelming the model with everything - think 'right amount of context' not 'all the context'
Create custom scripts within skills for deterministic data analysis (specific formulas, column operations) rather than relying on the LLM's non-deterministic interpretation
Skills can be sold as products - there is an emerging marketplace for specialized Claude skills and plugins that others can install for their workflows
opportunities: Marketplace for industry-specific Claude Skills (paid media analysis, financial reporting, legal document review)
Claude Skills consulting service that audits enterprise workflows and builds custom skill libraries for go-to-market teams
quotes: AI is essentially your coworker or someone that reports to you. You want to train it. You want to build the guidelines and limit the constraints around it.

2025-11-10 - Glif AI: The $10 App That R.episode_key: 2025-11-10 - Glif AI: The $10 App That Replaces a Full Creative Team
2025-11-10 - Glif AI: The $10 App That R.title: Glif AI: The $10 App That Replaces a Full Creative Team
2025-11-10 - Glif AI: The $10 App That R.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg explores Glif AI, a $10 creative AI platform that chains multiple models (Nano Banana, Kling, Wan 2.2, ElevenLabs) into agent workflows for non-technical users. Live demos include redesigning YouTube thumbnails in Mr. Beast style, creating 40-second miniature history documentaries with AI-generated images, voiceover, and music, and building AI influencer talking-head videos. The broader thesis: brands should create 10 creator accounts (mix of real and AI) rather than one brand account, using tools like Glif to scale content production.
key_takeaways: The missing step in creative AI is prompt optimization - agent workflows that analyze your input and generate model-specific prompts dramatically improve output quality
Build multiple creator accounts (real + AI influencers) per brand rather than one brand account - if even 1-2 of 10 accounts pop, you gain massive distribution
Follow the ACP funnel: Audience first (creative AI content), then Community (paid groups, IRL events), then Product (vibe code and sell to your audience)
opportunities: AI influencer creation agency that builds and manages multiple AI personality accounts for brands across Instagram and TikTok
Creative AI workflow marketplace where experts sell optimized multi-model pipelines for specific content types (thumbnails, ads, documentaries)
quotes: The people that could unleash creative AI have an unfair advantage because those are the people that are going to be able to create big audiences, ads that outperform, and vibe-coded websites that don't look like every other vibe-coded app.

2025-11-13 - Startup idea someone should.title: Startup idea someone should steal
2025-11-13 - Startup idea someone should.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg presents a new format covering one startup idea (female solo travel safety review platform), one trend (gamification in apps), one news analysis (ex-Reddit CEO's AI bear thesis rebuttal), one growth framework (creator + affiliate + gamification), and one AI tool (Crea AI). The standout idea is a map-based platform where verified female travelers rate neighborhoods on safety metrics, monetized via subscriptions and hotel safety certifications. Greg argues against the thesis that foundation model companies will crush all AI application startups.
key_takeaways: Build a value ladder for your startup: free quiz (email capture) to community access ($10/mo) to premium subscription ($30/mo) to B2B certification ($500/mo) - each step increases trust and price
Gamification is an underrated UI trend across apps and websites - there is an opportunity for a specialist agency focused solely on adding gamification layers to existing products
Use a 3-step growth framework: attach the right creator (shoot your shot with 100, focus on sub-120K followers), offer 30-50% lifetime affiliate commissions, and gamify the affiliate experience
opportunities: Safety-focused travel platform for women with map-based reviews, hotel certifications, and community features - 84% of solo female travelers report feeling unsafe
Gamification consulting agency that specializes in adding points, streaks, leaderboards, and challenges to SaaS and consumer apps
quotes: AI application startups that actually own the workflow, own the customer, and have network effects are going to be extremely valuable in the future.

2025-12-03 - 10 Unknown Apps Making $50K.title: 10 Unknown Apps Making $50K+ MRR (Copy Them)
2025-12-03 - 10 Unknown Apps Making $50K.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg reverse-engineers eight mobile apps making $50K+ MRR in the last 30 days, extracting replicable patterns: AI video generators (FlashLoop), religious note-taking apps, AI interior design tools, emoji/sticker creators, vinyl record scanners, multi-LLM assistants, logo makers, and healthy restaurant menu finders. Each breakdown covers why it works psychologically, its pricing model, and 3-5 adjacent niches where the same format could be applied. The episode concludes with six frameworks for ideating mobile app ideas.
key_takeaways: Apps succeeding at $50K+ MRR share common traits: they serve daily habits, have simple one-feature cores, leverage AI for personalization, and use ASO-optimized names
The most profitable mobile app niches combine identity expression (vanity), financial stakes (collectibles), or health anxiety (allergies, calories) with high-frequency usage patterns
Simple names dominate ASO: 'Logo Maker' does $200K/month because people literally search 'logo maker' in the App Store - name your app what people search for
opportunities: AI-powered collectibles scanner apps for specific verticals (comic books, sports cards, vintage clothing) with instant valuation and marketplace integration
Niche AI assistant apps that bundle multiple LLMs for specific professional workflows (student research, sales CRM, legal document analysis)
quotes: Now is the greatest time to be building mobile apps. And there are people printing doing it.

2025-12-05 - With Harvey Co-Founder Gabe.title: With Harvey Co-Founder Gabe Pereyra
2025-12-05 - With Harvey Co-Founder Gabe.executive_summary: Harvey co-founder Gabe Pereyra discusses building AI for the legal industry, now approaching 1,000 customers and 500 employees. The conversation covers Harvey's evolution from individual lawyer productivity to firm-wide organizational transformation, the analogy between legal reasoning traces and coding RL environments, and why Harvey chose to build a platform for all law firms rather than compete as a law firm itself. Pereyra draws parallels between distinguished engineers and senior law partners, emphasizing that the real value lies in decade-long pattern recognition that current models cannot replicate.
key_takeaways: The shift from individual productivity tools to organizational productivity platforms represents the next frontier - making a lawyer 20% faster does not make a law firm 20% more productive
Legal RL environments using partner feedback and internal editing history as reward signals offer a promising path for training domain-specific reasoning models
Forward-deployed engineering teams are essential for large enterprise AI adoption - the value is not just the product but helping firms transform their business operations
opportunities: Professional services collaboration platforms that enable secure data sharing and AI-powered workflows across law firms, investment banks, and consultancies
RL training data businesses that capture expert decision-making processes (reasoning traces) in knowledge-intensive professions
quotes: When we first got access to GPT-4, Winston went in his room for 14 hours and just redid a bunch of his associate tasks. And what I saw was essentially hacky auto-agentic work.

2025-12-11 - With ElevenLabs Co-Founder.title: With ElevenLabs Co-Founder Mati Staniszewski
2025-12-11 - With ElevenLabs Co-Founder.executive_summary: ElevenLabs co-founder Mati Staniszewski discusses the company's growth to $300M ARR and 350 employees, split roughly 50/50 between self-serve creative tools and enterprise agent platforms. Key themes include voice as the future interface for all devices and robots, the architectural advantage of cascaded vs. fused speech models, and why ElevenLabs' research moat comes from 10 of the world's top 50-100 audio researchers rather than compute scale. Notable deployments include Epic Games (Darth Vader in Fortnite), chess.com (learn from Magnus Carlsen), MasterClass (practice negotiations with Chris Voss), and Ukraine's agentic government initiative.
key_takeaways: Voice AI is shifting from reactive customer support to proactive customer experience - the biggest opportunity is making voice agents the front door of e-commerce and service discovery

Research advantages in audio AI come from architectural breakthroughs by a small number of elite researchers, not compute scale - but models will eventually commoditize, making the product layer essential
Education will be the largest voice AI use case: personalized AI tutors that understand individual learners, delivered through headphones, with the voices of iconic teachers
opportunities: Voice-first e-commerce experiences where AI agents guide customers from product discovery through checkout using personalized voice interactions
AI-powered education platforms with celebrity/expert voice clones for personalized tutoring across languages
quotes: In the ideal world in the future you will be able to have content delivered naturally around languages still carrying the original voice, original emotions, original intonation.

2025-12-19 - The 2026 AI Forecast with S.episode_key: 2025-12-19 - The 2026 AI Forecast with Sarah and Elad
2025-12-19 - The 2026 AI Forecast with S.title: The 2026 AI Forecast with Sarah and Elad
2025-12-19 - The 2026 AI Forecast with S.executive_summary: Sarah Guo and Elad Gil deliver their 2026 AI predictions with guest contributions from industry leaders. Key forecasts: next wave of vertical AI consolidation (beyond coding/legal/medical), digital biology's ChatGPT moment for protein generation, robotics hype cycle correction, more AI IPOs with strong retail demand, breakthrough consumer AI experiences, and defense tech acceleration. The episode pushes back on overstated bubble narratives, noting that the slowest technology adopters (doctors, lawyers) are actually the fastest AI adopters. Guest predictions cover proactive AI agents, context as the key product differentiator, and the year YOLO dies.
key_takeaways: The next set of vertical AI companies will consolidate in 2026 - look for the domain equivalent of what Cursor did for coding in industries like accounting, nursing, and compliance
Digital biology is approaching its generative AI moment: protein generation, chemical understanding, and cell foundation models will create breakthrough applications
Defense tech is accelerating structurally, not just as hype - the shift to autonomous drone-based warfare and the need for domestic supply chains creates lasting demand
opportunities: Vertical AI applications in professional domains with severe labor shortages (accounting, nursing, compliance, insurance)
AI-driven materials science and drug discovery companies leveraging new foundation models for molecular design
quotes: The people who tended to be the slowest adopters of technology love AI. Physicians, lawyers, certain accounting types - it's actually kind of fascinating.

2026-01-05 - Watch me use AI to make mil.episode_key: 2026-01-05 - Watch me use AI to make millions in ecommerce
2026-01-05 - Watch me use AI to make mil.title: Watch me use AI to make millions in ecommerce
2026-01-05 - Watch me use AI to make mil.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg reviews Alibaba's Axio AI agent platform, which combines trend analysis, product design generation, supplier matching, and margin calculation into a single e-commerce research workflow. The demo covers identifying rising trends in baby products, generating Squid Game merchandise concepts with AI-designed mockups, finding verified manufacturers with pricing, and analyzing unmet customer pain points in mechanical keyboards using Amazon reviews and Reddit forums. Only single-digit companies have crossed $10B in pure-play software revenue because building a true platform (multiple integrated products, deep system integrations, enterprise-grade compliance) is exceptionally rare. He argues that vibe-coded apps increase application velocity but don't solve enterprise requirements like multi-cloud resiliency, regulatory compliance, and air-gapped networks. MongoDB's structural advantage: unstructured data storage is the one constant in the AI stack.
key_takeaways: Platforms are sticky, products are not - enterprise customers with 300+ applications built on your platform become structurally locked in, creating durable competitive advantage
The SaaS bear thesis is overblown: while individual products can be replaced, the data layer and LLM layer will always be required in any AI application stack

opportunities: AI-native SaaS companies that position as full system-of-record replacements (not add-on layers) with disruptive pricing and faster speed-to-value than incumbents

Template marketplace for AI-generated Reel formats optimized for specific niches (food, fitness, tech reviews)
quotes: The key to viral Reels is not one perfect video - it's using AI to test 30 variations and double down on what the algorithm rewards.

2026-03-06 - Cursors Third Era Cloud Age.title: Cursors Third Era Cloud Agents
2026-03-06 - Cursors Third Era Cloud Age.executive_summary: Cursor team members discuss the company's evolution into its 'third era' of cloud-based AI agents that can autonomously execute complex software engineering tasks in remote environments. The conversation covers how cloud agents differ from in-IDE copilots, the technical challenges of giving AI agents full development environment access, and Cursor's vision for the future of software development where agents handle entire feature implementations.
key_takeaways: Cloud agents represent a paradigm shift from AI-assisted coding (suggestions in your IDE) to AI-executed coding (agents that independently build features in cloud environments)
The key technical challenge is giving agents enough context and tool access to work autonomously while maintaining security and code quality guardrails
The future of software development will involve humans defining specifications and reviewing agent-produced code rather than writing it line by line
opportunities: Cloud development environments optimized for AI agent execution with built-in sandboxing, testing, and code review capabilities
Specification-to-code platforms where product managers describe features in natural language and AI agents implement them end-to-end

2026-03-09 - OpenClaws Memory Sucks and.episode_key: 2026-03-09 - OpenClaws Memory Sucks and the fix is simple
2026-03-09 - OpenClaws Memory Sucks and.title: OpenClaws Memory Sucks and the fix is simple
2026-03-09 - OpenClaws Memory Sucks and.executive_summary: Supermemory founder Dhravya Shah diagnoses the memory problem in AI coding agents like OpenClaw, arguing that current memory systems are too simplistic (append-only logs) to support effective long-running agent sessions. The proposed fix involves structured memory with hierarchical organization, relevance scoring, and automatic pruning - drawing from how human memory works with short-term, working, and long-term memory systems.
key_takeaways: Current AI agent memory systems (simple append-only context) degrade performance over long sessions as irrelevant information crowds out important context
Effective agent memory requires hierarchical structure: short-term (current task), working (active project), and long-term (organizational knowledge) with automatic relevance scoring
Memory management is becoming a critical infrastructure layer for AI agents - the agents that remember effectively will dramatically outperform those that don't
opportunities: Memory infrastructure for AI agents that provides hierarchical, structured context management with automatic relevance scoring and pruning
Agent memory-as-a-service platforms that any AI agent framework can plug into for persistent, intelligent context management
quotes: OpenClaw's memory sucks and the fix is simple - stop treating memory as an append-only log and start treating it like human memory with structure and relevance.

2026-03-09 - I gave OpenClaw one job go.episode_key: 2026-03-09 - I gave OpenClaw one job go viral (it worked)
2026-03-09 - I gave OpenClaw one job go.title: I gave OpenClaw one job go viral (it worked)
2026-03-09 - I gave OpenClaw one job go.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg experiments with OpenClaw, tasking the AI agent with a single objective: make content go viral. The episode documents the agent's autonomous research into trending topics, content format analysis, hook optimization, and multi-platform distribution strategy. The results demonstrate that AI agents can effectively handle the full content creation and distribution pipeline when given clear success metrics.
key_takeaways: AI agents given a clear success metric (virality) can autonomously research trends, create optimized content, and develop distribution strategies across platforms
The key to effective agent tasking is defining the outcome metric clearly rather than specifying the process - let the agent figure out the how
Current AI agents are most effective for content tasks where success is measurable (views, engagement) versus subjective quality assessments
opportunities: AI agent-powered content marketing services where clients define success metrics and agents autonomously execute the full creation-to-distribution pipeline
Agent performance benchmarking platforms that compare different AI agents on real-world content creation and marketing tasks
quotes: I gave OpenClaw one job - go viral. And it worked. The agent autonomously researched trends, created content, and developed the distribution strategy.

2026-03-11 - Karpathy's autoresearch bro.title: Karpathy's autoresearch broke the internet
2026-03-11 - Karpathy's autoresearch bro.executive_summary: Greg Isenberg discusses Andrej Karpathy's autoresearch project that went viral, demonstrating how AI agents can autonomously conduct research, synthesize findings, and generate comprehensive reports. The episode explores the implications for knowledge workers, researchers, and entrepreneurs who can now leverage AI to conduct deep research in hours rather than weeks.
key_takeaways: Autonomous research agents that can navigate the web, synthesize multiple sources, and generate structured reports represent a paradigm shift for knowledge work
The democratization of deep research capabilities means that solo founders and small teams can now compete with large organizations on insight quality
The key differentiator is not access to research tools but the quality of questions asked and the ability to act on research findings quickly
opportunities: AI-powered research-as-a-service for specific industries (market research, competitive intelligence, academic literature review)
Research agent platforms that allow non-technical users to define research objectives and receive comprehensive, cited reports
quotes: Karpathy's autoresearch project showed that what used to take a research team weeks can now be done by an AI agent in hours.

2026-03-11 - Bioinfohazards Jassi Pannu.episode_key: 2026-03-11 - Bioinfohazards Jassi Pannu on Controlling Dangerous Data
2026-03-11 - Bioinfohazards Jassi Pannu.title: Bioinfohazards Jassi Pannu on Controlling Dangerous Data
2026-03-11 - Bioinfohazards Jassi Pannu.executive_summary: Jassi Pannu discusses the challenge of bioinfohazards - dangerous biological information that could be misused if widely accessible. The conversation covers the tension between open science and biosecurity, how AI models trained on biological data could lower barriers to creating dangerous pathogens, and what governance frameworks are needed to manage information hazards in the age of AI-powered biology.
key_takeaways: AI models trained on biological data could inadvertently lower the knowledge barriers for creating dangerous biological agents, requiring new information governance frameworks
The tension between open science (which accelerates beneficial research) and biosecurity (which requires restricting dangerous knowledge) has no easy resolution
Practical governance approaches include structured access models, responsible disclosure frameworks, and AI model evaluations specifically testing for dual-use biological capabilities
opportunities: Biosecurity evaluation platforms that test AI models for dangerous biological knowledge and capabilities before deployment
Structured access systems for sensitive biological databases that enable legitimate research while preventing misuse
quotes: The challenge with bioinfohazards is that the same information that enables life-saving research can also enable catastrophic misuse.

key_takeaways: The transition from note-taking to AI workspace requires rethinking the product around AI-native interactions rather than bolting AI onto existing features
Notion's advantage is its structured knowledge base that provides rich context for AI - the quality of AI outputs is directly proportional to the quality of organized information it can access
The productivity software market is being restructured by AI: tools that can serve as both the knowledge layer and the AI interaction layer will capture the most value
opportunities: AI-native workspace platforms that combine knowledge management, task automation, and AI interaction in a unified interface
Enterprise knowledge graph solutions that structure organizational information to maximize the quality of AI-powered workflows
quotes: The future of productivity tools is not AI as a feature - it's the entire workspace reimagined around AI as the primary way you interact with your team's knowledge.

2026-03-14 - Monty the ultrafast Python.episode_key: 2026-03-14 - Monty the ultrafast Python interpreter by Agents for Agents
2026-03-14 - Monty the ultrafast Python.title: Monty the ultrafast Python interpreter by Agents for Agents
2026-03-14 - Monty the ultrafast Python.executive_summary: Latent Space covers Monty, an ultrafast Python interpreter specifically designed for AI agents rather than human developers. The episode explores why existing Python interpreters are too slow for agentic workflows that need to execute thousands of code snippets rapidly, how Monty optimizes for the specific patterns of AI-generated code, and the emerging infrastructure layer of tools built by agents for agents.
key_takeaways: AI agents generate and execute code differently than humans - they need interpreters optimized for rapid execution of many small snippets rather than long-running programs
The 'tools for agents' infrastructure layer is an emerging category: agents need specialized runtimes, sandboxes, and execution environments different from human developer tools
Performance optimization at the interpreter level has multiplicative effects on agent capabilities since faster code execution enables more iterations and more complex reasoning chains
opportunities: Agent-optimized runtime environments and sandboxes that provide fast, secure code execution for AI coding agents
Infrastructure tooling specifically designed for AI agent workflows rather than human developer workflows
quotes: Monty is built by agents for agents - the realization that AI agents need fundamentally different tools than human developers is creating an entire new infrastructure layer.

2026-03-16 - AI Scouting Report the Good.executive_summary: The Cognitive Revolution provides a scouting report on AI adoption in the legal industry, covering what is working well, what is failing, and what unexpected developments have emerged. The analysis examines specific use cases from document review to contract drafting, the regulatory landscape for AI in legal practice, and how law firms are reorganizing around AI capabilities. The episode highlights the gap between vendor promises and practical deployment reality.
key_takeaways: AI excels at legal document review and research but still struggles with nuanced legal reasoning and strategy - the best results come from human-AI collaboration rather than full automation
The biggest barrier to legal AI adoption is not technology but change management - law firms that invest in training and workflow redesign see dramatically better ROI

The discussion covers the technical architecture, security implications, and product design decisions behind Anthropic's computer use capabilities, and what this means for the future of AI agents that can interact with the digital world like humans do.
key_takeaways: Giving AI its own computer environment enables a fundamentally different class of tasks: multi-step workflows that require navigating applications, filling forms, and interacting with web services
The security architecture for AI computer use requires sandboxing, permission systems, and audit trails that don't exist in traditional computing environments
Computer use capabilities transform AI from a text-in/text-out system to a general-purpose digital worker that can automate any workflow a human can perform on a computer
opportunities: Secure sandboxed environments optimized for AI computer use with built-in permissions, monitoring, and rollback capabilities

quotes: Giving AI its own computer is not just a feature - it's a paradigm shift that transforms AI from an advisor into a digital worker that can actually do things.

2023-08-03 - FlashAttention-2 Making Tra.executive_summary: Tri Dao discusses FlashAttention-2, achieving 2-4x speedup by optimizing memory I/O patterns using GPU memory hierarchy (SRAM vs HBM) and online softmax trick for kernel fusion.

Speed of iteration is a startup's only real advantage
Answer engines will become their own market segment

key_takeaways: Open source will reach GPT-3.5 level within a year
Regulation risks following nuclear model that blocked innovation 50 years
Enterprise retrieval/personalization for LLMs is a major missing layer
opportunities: AI voice dubbing platform
Compliance automation tools
quotes: The future is here, it's just not equally distributed

2023-05-01 - No Priors Ep. 2 With Runwa.title: No Priors Ep. 2  With Runway MLÆs Cristobal Valenzuela
2023-05-01 - No Priors Ep. 2 With Runwa.executive_summary: Runway ML CEO discusses evolving from model directory to applied research company building AI creative tools for filmmakers. Human-in-the-loop design is crucial for creative domains where 80% accuracy suffices.

80% accuracy is useful in creative domains unlike safety-critical
Multi-modal systems will transform creative workflows

2023-05-03 - No Priors Ep. 3 With Stabi.title: No Priors Ep. 3  With Stability AIÆs Emad Mostaque
2023-05-03 - No Priors Ep. 3 With Stabi.executive_summary: Stability AI CEO discusses democratizing AI through open source across images, language, audio, protein folding. Foundation models should be public infrastructure. Small model ensembles will outperform large monolithic ones.
key_takeaways: Open source AI has broader compute access via national supercomputers
Small combined models can outperform single massive models
Visual communication becoming easy is comparable to printing press
opportunities: Open source medical AI for developing nations
AI education tablets for emerging markets
quotes: Small models will outperform large models through hive approach

2023-05-04 - No Priors Ep. 15 With Kelv.episode_key: 2023-05-04 - No Priors Ep. 15  With Kelvin Guu, Staff Research Scientist, Google Brain
2023-05-04 - No Priors Ep. 15 With Kelv.title: No Priors Ep. 15  With Kelvin Guu, Staff Research Scientist, Google Brain
2023-05-04 - No Priors Ep. 15 With Kelv.executive_summary: Google Brain researcher discusses retrieval augmented models (REALM), instruction following evolution, and autonomous agents. Model surgery can edit factual knowledge. Contextually available AI assistants are the next frontier.
key_takeaways: RAG is most compelling for modularity and enterprise customization
Model surgery edits facts while propagating related knowledge
Agents need instinct-like mechanisms beyond explicit reasoning
opportunities: Enterprise RAG platforms
Contextual browser AI assistants
quotes: The differentiating factor shifts to problem formulation and creativity

2023-05-10 - No Priors Ep. 4 With Zipli.title: No Priors Ep. 4  With ZiplineÆs Keller Rinaudo Cliffton
2023-05-10 - No Priors Ep. 4 With Zipli.executive_summary: Zipline CEO describes building world's largest autonomous delivery system from Kickstarter robots to nationwide healthcare delivery in Rwanda. The drone is only 15% of complexity - operations make up the rest.
key_takeaways: Drone is 15% of solution complexity - operations and logistics are 85%
Starting with healthcare made regulatory and economic dimensions easier
Acoustic detect-and-avoid using microphones solved 20-year aviation problem
opportunities: Autonomous drone delivery for healthcare
Teleportation combining telemedicine with instant delivery
quotes: Assume we are idiots because we are proven correct more often than not

2023-05-11 - No Priors Ep. 16 With Must.episode_key: 2023-05-11 - No Priors Ep. 16  With Mustafa Suleyman, Founder of DeepMind and Inflection
2023-05-11 - No Priors Ep. 16 With Must.title: No Priors Ep. 16  With Mustafa Suleyman, Founder of DeepMind and Inflection
2023-05-11 - No Priors Ep. 16 With Must.executive_summary: DeepMind co-founder discusses founding Inflection and launching Pi personal AI. Billions of specialized AIs will exist. Conversation is the future interface. Personal AIs will act as interlocutors accessing other AIs for users.
key_takeaways: Router between specialized models more important than any single large model
Every individual will have their own personal AI interlocutor
Conversation is the future interface replacing old search paradigms
opportunities: Personal AI companion platform
Dynamic personalized content replacing static websites
quotes: Google is already a conversation, just an appallingly painful one

2023-05-18 - No Priors Ep. 17 With Kara.episode_key: 2023-05-18 - No Priors Ep. 17  With Karan Singhal
2023-05-18 - No Priors Ep. 17 With Kara.title: No Priors Ep. 17  With Karan Singhal
2023-05-18 - No Priors Ep. 17 With Kara.executive_summary: Google researcher discusses MedPaLM 2 and responsible generative AI in healthcare. Medical AI is ideal laboratory for safety research. As AI approaches physician-level performance, scalable oversight becomes critical.
key_takeaways: Medical AI is ideal for safety research - stakes force oversight solutions
Instruction prompt tuning provides data-efficient medical alignment
Telling AI from physicians apart is becoming impossible at current performance
opportunities: AI radiology assistant
Medical literature synthesis for physicians
quotes: 10% of internet searches are for health information
